_matchRepository;\n        private readonly UserMockRepository _userRepository;\n        private readonly ClubMockRepository _clubRepository;\n\n        public RatingController(\n            RatingMockRepository ratingRepository,\n            PlayerMockRepository playerRepository,\n            MatchMockRepository matchRepository,\n            UserMockRepository userRepository,\n            ClubMockRepository clubRepository)\n        {\n            _ratingRepository = ratingRepository;\n            _playerRepository = playerRepository;\n            _matchRepository = matchRepository;\n            _userRepository = userRepository;\n            _clubRepository = clubRepository;\n        }\n\n        public IActionResult Index()\n        {\n            var ratings = _ratingRepository.GetAll();\n            return View(ratings);\n        }\n\n        public IActionResult Details(int id)\n        {\n            var rating = _ratingRepository.GetById(id);\n Id = rating.Id,\n                PlayerName = player != null ? $\"{player.FirstName} {player.LastName}\" : \"Unknown Player\",\n                MatchDescription = matchDescription,\n The UX sub-agent is responsible for all visual design, layout decisions, component styling, and ensuring a consistent, professional user experience across all pages.\n\n---\n\n## Design Philosophy\n\n### Core Principles\n- **Dark Modern**: Use dark backgrounds (charcoal, near-black) with bright, readable text (near-white, soft grays)\n- **Professional & Minimal**: Avoid clutter. Football context should enhance, not overwhelm\n- **Football-Inspired but Subtle**: Colors and imagery reference football without being cartoonish\n- **Accessible & Readable**: High contrast ratios, clear typography, generous spacing\n- **Consistent & Cohesive**: Same patterns repeat across all pages (players, clubs, matches, ratings)\n\n### Target Aesthetic\nThink modern sports dashboard (like Premier League website or ESPN's dark mode), not stadium-themed chaos.\n\n---\n\n## Color Palette\n\n### Primary Colors\n- **Background (Primary)**: `#0f172a` (dark slate, nearly black)\n- **Background (Secondary)**: `#1e293b` (slightly lighter slate for cards/containers)\n- **Accent Color**: `#10b981` (emerald green ÔÇô represents football pitch)\n- **Accent Hover**: `#059669` (darker emerald for interactivity)\n\n### Text Colors\n- **Primary Text**: `#f1f5f9` (soft white, very light slate)\n- **Secondary Text**: `#cbd5e1` (muted gray for metadata/timestamps)\n- **Muted Text**: `#94a3b8` (dimmer gray for labels)\n\n### Supporting Colors\n- **Success**: `#10b981` (emerald ÔÇô matches accent)\n- **Warning**: `#f59e0b` (amber ÔÇô for scores/highlights)\n- **Danger**: `#ef4444` (red ÔÇô for red cards/errors)\n- **Info**: `#3b82f6` (blue ÔÇô for neutral information)\n\n### Highlight/Focus\n- **Focus State**: `#22d3ee` (cyan ÔÇô for interactive elements)\n- **Border Color**: `#334155` (dark gray for subtle dividers)\n\n---\n\n## Typography\n\n### Font Stack\n```css\nfont-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', 'Roboto', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;\n```\n(Tailwind default is fine)\n\n### Hierarchy\n- **Page Titles (h1)**: \n  - Size: `2.25rem` (36px) ÔÇô Tailwind `text-4xl`\n  - Weight: `700` (bold)\n  - Color: `#f1f5f9`\n  - Margin: `1.5rem bottom`\n\n- **Section Titles (h2)**:\n  - Size: `1.875rem` (30px) ÔÇô Tailwind `text-3xl`\n  - Weight: `600` (semibold)\n  - Color: `#f1f5f9`\n  - Margin: `1rem bottom`\n\n- **Subsection Titles (h3)**:\n  - Size: `1.5rem` (24px) ÔÇô Tailwind `text-2xl`\n  - Weight: `600`\n  - Color: `#cbd5e1`\n\n- **Body Text**:\n  - Size: `1rem` (16px)\n  - Weight: `400` (regular)\n  - Color: `#f1f5f9`\n  - Line Height: `1.6`\n\n- **Small/Metadata**:\n  - Size: `0.875rem` (14px) ÔÇô Tailwind `text-sm`\n  - Color: `#cbd5e1`\n\n---\n\n## Layout & Spacing\n\n### Grid System\n- Use Tailwind's **12-column grid** for consistency\n- **Container max-width**: `max-w-7xl` (80rem)\n- **Padding**: \n  - Page/container: `2rem` (32px) padding on all sides\n  - Card internal: `1.5rem` (24px)\n  - Between sections: `2-3rem` (32-48px gap)\n\n### Responsive Breakpoints (use Tailwind defaults)\n- Mobile: base (no prefix)\n- Tablet: `md:` (768px)\n- Desktop: `lg:` (1024px)\n- Large: `xl:` (1280px)\n\n### Spacing Unit\nAll spacing uses multiples of `0.25rem` (4px):\n- `p-2` = 8px, `p-4` = 16px, `p-6` = 24px, `m-4` = 16px, etc.\n\n---\n\n## Components\n\n### Navigation Bar (_Layout.cshtml)\n- **Background**: `bg-slate-900` (`#0f172a`)\n- **Height**: `h-16` (64px)\n- **Border**: Subtle bottom border `border-b border-slate-700` \n- **Logo/Brand**: Left-aligned, `text-2xl font-bold text-emerald-400`\n- **Nav Links**: \n  - Spacing: `mx-2` or `mx-3` between links\n  - Color: `text-slate-200` (default)\n  - Hover: `hover:text-emerald-400 cursor-pointer transition-colors`\n  - Active: `text-emerald-400 border-b-2 border-emerald-400`\n- **Dropdown/Mobile menu** (if responsive): Dark background, same color scheme\n\n### Cards (for lists/index pages)\n```css\n.card {\n  background: #1e293b;         /* bg-slate-800 */\n  border: 1px solid #334155;   /* border-slate-700 */\n  border-radius: 0.5rem;       /* rounded-lg */\n  padding: 1.5rem;             /* p-6 */\n  transition: all 0.3s ease;   /* smooth hover effect */\n}\n\n.card:hover {\n  background: #0f172a;         /* slightly darker on hover */\n  border-color: #10b981;       /* emerald accent */\n  box-shadow: 0 10px 25px rgba(16, 185, 129, 0.1); /* subtle emerald glow */\n}\n```\n\n### Buttons\n- **Default Button**:\n  - Background: `bg-emerald-600`\n  - Text: `text-white`\n  - Padding: `px-4 py-2` (16px +ů 8px)\n  - Rounded: `rounded-md`\n  - Hover: `hover:bg-emerald-700 transition-colors`\n  - Focus: `ring-2 ring-emerald-500 ring-offset-2 ring-offset-slate-900`\n\n- **Secondary Button**:\n  - Background: `bg-slate-700`\n  - Text: `text-slate-100`\n  - Border: `border border-slate-600`\n  - Hover: `hover:bg-slate-600`\n\n- **Danger Button**:\n  - Background: `bg-red-600`\n  - Hover: `hover:bg-red-700`\n\n### Tables (for index views)\n```css\n.table {\n  width: 100%;\n  border-collapse: collapse;\n}\n\n.table thead {\n  background: #1e293b;    /* bg-slate-800 */\n  border-bottom: 2px solid #334155; /* border-slate-700 */\n}\n\n.table th {\n  padding: 1rem;          /* p-4 */\n  text-align: left;\n  color: #cbd5e1;         /* text-slate-300 */\n  font-weight: 600;       /* font-semibold */\n  text-transform: uppercase;\n  font-size: 0.875rem;    /* text-sm */\n}\n\n.table tbody tr {\n  border-bottom: 1px solid #334155; /* border-slate-700 */\n  transition: background 0.2s;\n}\n\n.table tbody tr:hover {\n  background: #1e293b;    /* hover effect */\n}\n\n.table td {\n  padding: 1rem;\n  color: #f1f5f9;         /* text-slate-50 */\n}\n```\n\n### Badges (for player positions, match status)\n```css\n.badge {\n  display: inline-block;\n  padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem; /* px-3 py-1 */\n  background: #3b82f6;   /* bg-blue-500 (default) */\n  color: white;\n  border-radius: 9999px; /* rounded-full */\n  font-size: 0.875rem;   /* text-sm */\n  font-weight: 500;      /* font-medium */\n}\n\n.badge.position-gk { background: #6366f1; }      /* Goalkeeper - indigo */\n.badge.position-def { background: #10b981; }     /* Defender - emerald */\n.badge.position-mid { background: #f59e0b; }     /* Midfielder - amber */\n.badge.position-fwd { background: #ef4444; }     /* Forward - red */\n```\n\n### Forms & Inputs\n- **Input Field**:\n  - Background: `bg-slate-800`\n  - Border: `border border-slate-700`\n  - Text: `text-slate-50`\n  - Padding: `px-3 py-2`\n  - Rounded: `rounded-md`\n  - Focus: `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-emerald-500 focus:border-emerald-500`\n\n### Alert/Message Boxes\n- **Success**: `bg-emerald-900 text-emerald-100 border border-emerald-700`\n- **Error**: `bg-red-900 text-red-100 border border-red-700`\n- **Warning**: `bg-amber-900 text-amber-100 border border-amber-700`\n- **Info**: `bg-blue-900 text-blue-100 border border-blue-700`\n\n---\n\n## Page-Specific Guidelines\n\n### Dashboard / Home Page\n- **Hero Section**: Large title with subtitle, background gradient (dark slate to slightly lighter)\n- **Stats Cards**: 3-4 key stats (total matches, top player, recent score) in grid layout\n- **Recent Activity**: Last 5-10 matches in a timeline or compact table\n- **League Overview**: Small table or cards showing league standings\n- **Call-to-Action**: Prominent link to browse players/matches\n\n### Index Pages (Players, Clubs, Matches, Ratings, Users)\n- **Page Header**: Title + count of items\n- **Filter/Search Bar** (if added later): Above the list\n- **Cards or Table**: Display all items, clickable rows/cards link to Details\n- **Pagination** (optional): If more than 10 items\n\n### Details Pages\n- **Header Section**: Large title with badge/status\n- **Key Information**: 2-3 columns of data with clear labels\n- **Related Data**: Links to related entities (e.g., matches for a player, players for a club)\n- **Breadcrumb Navigation**: `Home > Entities > Entity Name`\n- **Action Links**: Link back to list, link to related entities\n\n---\n\n## Interaction & Transitions\n\n- **Hover States**: Subtle color shift or border color change (300ms transition)\n- **Focus States**: Ring outline (2px) in accent color\n- **Active States**: Bolder text or underline for navigation\n- **Loading States** (if applicable): Spinner or skeleton loaders with dark background\n- **Smooth Transitions**: Use `transition-all duration-300` for most interactions\n\n---\n\n## Dark Mode Do's & Don'ts\n\n### ÔŁŕ DO\n- Use sufficient contrast (WCAG AA minimum 4.5:1 for normal text)\n- Include subtle borders/dividers to separate sections\n- Use color accents sparingly (emerald for primary actions)\n- Make clickable elements obvious (hover/focus states)\n- Keep backgrounds consistently dark (don't mix too many shades)\n\n### ÔŘî DON'T\n- Use pure black (`#000000`) ÔÇô too harsh; use `#0f172a` or `#1e293b`\n- Use pure white (`#ffffff`) ÔÇô too bright; use `#f1f5f9`\n- Overuse ornaments or images; keep it clean\n- Hide interactive elements (buttons, links must be obvious)\n- Use very small fonts; minimum `text-base` for body content\n- Mix too many accent colors (emerald is primary; others are supporting)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy for Sub-Agent\n\nWhen invoking this UX sub-agent, provide:\n\n1. **Specific Page/Component**: \"Update the player index view to use cards instead of a table\"\n2. **Context**: Include relevant controllers/view file paths\n3. **Current State**: Share the existing CSS or HTML if modifications\n4. **Expected Output**: Clarify if you want Tailwind utility classes or custom CSS\n5. **Constraints**: Any restrictions on libraries or file locations\n\n### Example Prompt for UX Sub-Agent:\n```\nUpdate Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml:\n- Replace Bootstrap navbar with a dark-themed Tailwind navbar\n- Use the color palette: dark bg (#0f172a), text (#f1f5f9), accent emerald (#10b981)\n- Add smooth hover transitions on nav links\n- Keep responsive (mobile menu if needed)\nFollow the UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md file for exact color codes and component patterns.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Tailwind Setup (CDN Method ÔÇô Simplest)\n\nAdd to `_Layout.cshtml` `<head>`:\n```html\n<script src=\"https://cdn.tailwindcss.com\"></script>\n```\n\nFor custom colors, add a `<script>` block in `<head>` before Tailwind script (or use `tailwind.config.js` if building):\n```html\n<script>\n  tailwind.config = {\n    theme: {\n      extend: {\n        colors: {\n          'slate': { 50: '#f8fafc', 100: '#f1f5f9', /* ... existing tailwind colors ... */, 900: '#0f172a' }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n</script>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Git & Documentation\n\nThis instruction file should be:\n1. **Committed to Git** as `UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md`\n2. **Referenced in chat**: Link or quote relevant sections when asking for UI updates\n3. **Iterated on**: Update if design needs change or new patterns emerge\n4. **Logged**: Save conversation logs showing sub-agent invocation for Lab 2 proof\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria\n\nThe UX agent has succeeded when:\n- ÔŁŕ All pages use consistent dark color scheme\n- ÔŁŕ Navigation is clear and intuitive\n- ÔŁŕ Cards/tables are properly styled and highlighted\n- ÔŁŕ Buttons and forms are accessible (high contrast, clear focus states)\n- ÔŁŕ Text is readable (no low-contrast color combinations)\n- ÔŁŕ Design feels modern and professional (not default Bootstrap)\n- ÔŁŕ Football context is subtle but present (emerald pitch-inspired accent)\n- ÔŁŕ All entity pages (Players, Clubs, Matches, Ratings, Users) follow the same pattern\n- ÔŁŕ Dashboard home page showcases key information with visual hierarchy\n\n"},"tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_019QnhPDuMbEdDR53srxFBhp__vscode-1776246849784","cwd":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\Desktop\\Nuklearke\\FutScores"}

Remove the `box-shadow` and other Bootstrap-specific classes\n   - The navbar should be fully responsive (mobile-first approach)\n\n5. **Output**:\n   - Provide the complete updated `_Layout.cshtml` with the new navbar\n   - Include the Tailwind CDN script tag in `<head>`\n   - Keep the rest of the layout intact (footer, scripts, RenderBody, etc.)\n   - Make sure asp-area, asp-controller, asp-action Tag Helpers are preserved exactly\n\n## Design Reference\nReview this section from UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md:\n\n**Navigation Bar (_Layout.cshtml)**\n- **Background**: `bg-slate-900` (`#0f172a`)\n- **Height**: `h-16` (64px)\n- **Border**: Subtle bottom border `border-b border-slate-700` \n- **Logo/Brand**: Left-aligned, `text-2xl font-bold text-emerald-400`\n- **Nav Links**: \n  - SVG icons\n            const openIcon = menuBtn.querySelector('svg:first-of-type');\n            const closeIcon = menuBtn.querySelector('svg:last-of-type');\n document.querySelectorAll('#mobile-menu a').forEach(link => {\n            link.addEventListener('click', function() {\n document.querySelector('#mobile-menu-btn svg:last-of-type');\n                openIcon.classList.remove('hidden');\n                closeIcon.classList.add('hidden');\n            });\n        });\n {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T17:35:06.703Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"29143273-ec05-45b8-a17e-a99e796e60c4","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\d4c18c7a570ea7e20b73aa5979c086e8\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\29143273-ec05-45b8-a17e-a99e796e60c4.jsonl","tool_name":"runSubagent","tool_input":{"agentName":"Explore","description":"Style all entity views with dark theme","prompt":"You are the UX sub-agent for the FutScores football platform. Your job is to transform ALL entity views (Index and Details pages for Player, Club, League, Match, Rating, User) using the design system defined in UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md.\n\n## Current State\nAll views currently use basic HTML:\n- Index views: Simple HTML tables with border=\"1\"\n- Details views: Basic <p> tags with <strong> labels\n- No styling, no cards, no proper layout\n\n## Task\nTransform all 12 view files (6 entities +ů 2 views each) with the dark modern theme:\n\n### Index Pages (6 files):\n- **Player/Index.cshtml**: Convert table to cards grid, show player info with position badges\n- **Club/Index.cshtml**: Cards showing club name, founded date, league\n- **League/Index.cshtml**: Cards or styled table for leagues\n- **Match/Index.cshtml**: Cards showing match details (teams, score, date)\n- **Rating/Index.cshtml**: Cards showing player ratings with scores\n- **User/Index.cshtml**: Cards for user list\n\n### Details Pages (6 files):\n- **Player/Details.cshtml**: Structured layout with player info, position badge, club link\n- **Club/Details.cshtml**: Club info with league link, founded date\n- **League/Details.cshtml**: League info (may be simple)\n- **Match/Details.cshtml**: Match details with team links, score display\n- **Rating/Details.cshtml**: Rating details with links to player/match/user\n- **User/Details.cshtml**: User profile info\n\n## Design Requirements (from UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md):\n\n### Layout & Colors:\n- **Background**: `bg-slate-900` (primary dark)\n- **Cards**: `bg-slate-800` with `border-slate-700`, hover effects with emerald accent\n- **Text**: `text-slate-50` (primary), `text-slate-300` (secondary)\n- **Accent**: `text-emerald-400` for links and highlights\n- **Spacing**: Use Tailwind spacing units (p-6, m-4, etc.)\n\n### Index Page Structure:\n```html\n<div class=\"max-w-7xl mx-auto p-8\">\n  <div class=\"mb-8\">\n    <h1 class=\"text-4xl font-bold text-slate-50 mb-2\">Entity Name</h1>\n    <p class=\"text-slate-300\">X items found</p>\n  </div>\n  \n  <div class=\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6\">\n    <!-- Cards here -->\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Details Page Structure:\n```html\n<div class=\"max-w-7xl mx-auto p-8\">\n  <!-- Breadcrumb -->\n  <nav class=\"mb-6\">\n    <a href=\"/\" class=\"text-emerald-400 hover:text-emerald-300\">Home</a> > \n    <a href=\"/Entity\" class=\"text-emerald-400 hover:text-emerald-300\">Entities</a> > \n    <span class=\"text-slate-300\">Entity Name</span>\n  </nav>\n  \n  <!-- Header -->\n  <div class=\"mb-8\">\n    <h1 class=\"text-4xl font-bold text-slate-50 mb-2\">Entity Title</h1>\n    <div class=\"badge\"><!-- status/badge --></div>\n  </div>\n  \n  <!-- Content Grid -->\n  <div class=\"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-8\">\n    <div class=\"bg-slate-800 border border-slate-700 rounded-lg p-6\">\n      <!-- Key info -->\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"bg-slate-800 border border-slate-700 rounded-lg p-6\">\n      <!-- Related links -->\n    </div>\n  </div>\n  \n  <!-- Action buttons -->\n  <div class=\"mt-8\">\n    <a href=\"/Entity\" class=\"bg-emerald-600 hover:bg-emerald-700 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-md transition-colors\">Back to List</a>\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Card Component (for Index pages):\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-slate-800 border border-slate-700 rounded-lg p-6 hover:bg-slate-900 hover:border-emerald-500 transition-all duration-300 cursor-pointer\">\n  <h3 class=\"text-xl font-semibold text-slate-50 mb-2\">Entity Name</h3>\n  <p class=\"text-slate-300 mb-4\">Description/metadata</p>\n  <div class=\"flex justify-between items-center\">\n    <span class=\"badge\"><!-- position/status --></span>\n    <a href=\"/details/id\" class=\"text-emerald-400 hover:text-emerald-300 font-medium\">View Details ÔĺĆ</a>\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Badges (position, status):\n- Goalkeeper: `bg-indigo-500`\n- Defender: `bg-emerald-500` \n- Midfielder: `bg-amber-500`\n- Forward: `bg-red-500`\n\n## Specific Requirements:\n\n### Player Views:\n- **Index**: Cards with name, position badge, club name, nationality\n- **Details**: Photo placeholder, position badge, club link, stats\n\n### Club Views:\n- **Index**: Cards with name, founded year, league name\n- **Details**: Founded date, league link, players count\n\n### Match Views:\n- **Index**: Cards with Home vs Away, score, date, league\n- **Details**: Full match info, links to teams and league\n\n### Rating Views:\n- **Index**: Cards with player name, match description, score, user\n- **Details**: Full rating with links to all related entities\n\n## Output:\nProvide complete updated code for ALL 12 view files. Each file should:\n- Use Tailwind utility classes only\n- Follow the exact color palette from UX_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md\n- Be fully responsive (mobile-first)\n- Include proper semantic HTML\n- Have smooth hover transitions\n- Use the specified layout patterns\n\n## Constraints:\n- Keep all @model directives and ViewData[\"Title\"] assignments\n- Preserve all ASP.NET Tag Helpers (@Url.Action, etc.)\n- Use the exact ViewModel properties (don't change data binding)\n- No custom CSS - only Tailwind utilities\n- Make all cards/links clickable to navigate to details\n- Include breadcrumb navigation on all Details pages\n\nPlease provide the complete updated code for all 12 view files following the UX design system."},"tool_use_id":"call_76466189__vscode-1776246849799","cwd":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\Desktop\\Nuklearke\\FutScores"}

{"timestamp":"2026-05-03T11:38:26.414Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"5096774d-3717-4b07-aed8-78ef3d3a9316","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\b1d40f9c0e0244da0b4265dcb9db4d57\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\5096774d-3717-4b07-aed8-78ef3d3a9316.jsonl","tool_name":"apply_patch","tool_input":{"input":"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: c:\\Users\\Fran\\Desktop\\C#\\FutScores\\.agents\\skills\\ui-ux-discovery\\SKILL.md\n@@\n ---\n name: ui-ux-discovery\n-description: Describe what this skill does and when to use it. Include keywords that help agents identify relevant tasks.\n+description: Use this skill when defining UI and UX direction for an ASP.NET Core MVC app, planning a design system, interviewing the user about layout and visual preferences, creating a UX brief, identifying reusable components, or deciding how list, details, and form pages should look and behave. Keywords: UI, UX, design system, layout, dashboard, admin panel, list page, details page, form, cards, tables, navigation, responsive, Razor views, MVC.\n ---\n \n <!-- Tip: Use /create-skill in chat to generate content with agent assistance -->\n \n-Define the functionality provided by this skill, including detailed instructions and examples\n+# UI/UX Discovery\n+\n+Use this skill before generating UI code when the project does not yet have a clear visual or interaction direction.\n+\n+This skill helps the agent:\n+- interview the user about desired UI and UX direction\n+- inspect the existing MVC structure when needed\n+- identify reusable page patterns\n+- produce a concise UX brief\n+- prepare future UI skills for list, details, and form pages\n+\n+This skill is for discovery and planning first. It should not jump straight into implementation unless the user explicitly asks for code.\n+\n+## Use This Skill When\n+\n+Use this skill when:\n+- the app does not yet have a clear UI direction\n+- the user says they want to \"play with UI/UX ideas\" before coding\n+- the user wants a design system or reusable page patterns\n+- the user wants help choosing between dashboard, admin, sports, editorial, or minimal styles\n+- the user wants to standardize list pages, details pages, or forms\n+- the user wants the agent to ask questions first instead of inventing a design\n+\n+## Do Not Use This Skill When\n+\n+Do not use this skill when:\n+- the user already gave a complete visual brief and wants direct implementation\n+- the task is purely backend, EF, routing, or database related\n+- the user only wants a one-off CSS tweak with no design discussion\n+- the user asks for debugging unrelated UI code behavior\n+\n+## Primary Goal\n+\n+Your job is to turn vague UI wishes into a practical design brief that fits the existing ASP.NET Core MVC application.\n+\n+You must:\n+- ask a short, focused discovery interview\n+- separate confirmed preferences from your own recommendations\n+- keep recommendations grounded in the current project structure\n+- identify reusable components and page archetypes\n+- leave implementation for a later step unless explicitly requested\n+\n+## Discovery Workflow\n+\n+Follow this order.\n+\n+### 1. Understand Product Intent\n+\n+First determine what kind of application the user wants.\n+\n+Ask about:\n+- who the main user is\n+- what the most important tasks are\n+- whether the app should feel like an admin panel, sports dashboard, data browser, or showcase experience\n+- whether speed of use or visual presentation matters more\n+\n+### 2. Clarify Visual Direction\n+\n+Ask about:\n+- dark, light, or mixed theme\n+- compact or spacious layout\n+- tables or cards for list pages\n+- minimal or expressive design\n+- subtle or strong domain branding\n+- reference apps, screenshots, or styles they like\n+\n+### 3. Clarify Interaction Patterns\n+\n+Ask about:\n+- mobile priority versus desktop priority\n+- whether details pages should be compact or richer and more visual\n+- whether filters, search, badges, breadcrumbs, and stat cards are desirable\n+- whether forms should be standard CRUD forms or more guided experiences\n+\n+### 4. Inspect Existing Structure If Helpful\n+\n+If the workspace already contains views, layout files, or CSS, inspect them and align your recommendations with reality.\n+\n+Prefer checking:\n+- shared layout\n+- index pages\n+- details pages\n+- existing nav structure\n+- styling conventions\n+- current use of Bootstrap, Tailwind, CSS files, or partials\n+\n+### 5. Synthesize a UX Brief\n+\n+After the interview, produce a structured brief with these sections:\n+- Product Direction\n+- Users and Main Tasks\n+- Visual Direction\n+- Page Archetypes\n+- Reusable Components\n+- Interaction Principles\n+- Constraints\n+- Open Questions\n+- Recommended Next Step\n+\n+## Output Requirements\n+\n+Your output should be concise and structured.\n+\n+Always distinguish:\n+- Confirmed Preferences\n+- Inferred Recommendations\n+- Open Questions\n+\n+Avoid long generic design lectures.\n+\n+Prefer actionable conclusions like:\n+- use card-based details pages with stat blocks\n+- keep list pages table-first on desktop\n+- use consistent action bars across index and details pages\n+- standardize badges for domain labels like league or player position\n+\n+## Design Heuristics\n+\n+When making recommendations:\n+- prefer consistency over novelty\n+- respect the constraints of Razor views and MVC page composition\n+- recommend reusable partials when the same UI block will appear on multiple pages\n+- avoid proposing SPA-like interaction unless the user clearly wants it\n+- do not invent animations, themes, or layouts without user intent\n+- do not overload CRUD pages with visual noise\n+\n+## Expected Page Archetypes\n+\n+When relevant, organize findings around these page types:\n+- Dashboard\n+- List or Index Page\n+- Details Page\n+- Create or Edit Form\n+- Shared Layout and Navigation\n+\n+For each archetype, suggest:\n+- its job\n+- its information hierarchy\n+- the most suitable layout pattern\n+- which components should be reusable\n+\n+## Reusable Component Inventory\n+\n+When useful, identify a reusable component set such as:\n+- navbar\n+- page header\n+- action bar\n+- stat card\n+- data table\n+- entity card\n+- badge\n+- filter bar\n+- breadcrumb\n+- detail info grid\n+- form field group\n+- empty state\n+- error or success message\n+\n+## Good Behavior\n+\n+Good behavior looks like this:\n+- ask 6 to 8 focused questions before designing\n+- keep the questions practical and short\n+- summarize what the user wants before proposing structure\n+- adapt recommendations to the existing project\n+- make page-pattern recommendations that can be reused later in UI skills\n+\n+## Bad Behavior\n+\n+Bad behavior looks like this:\n+- immediately generating a full design without questions\n+- describing a generic trendy dashboard with no connection to the app\n+- mixing backend architecture advice into a UX discovery task\n+- over-prescribing exact colors and spacing before understanding the user\n+- pretending unclear preferences are already decided\n+\n+## Example Prompts\n+\n+Use this skill for prompts like:\n+- Help me define the UI direction for this MVC app before we build pages\n+- Ask me questions and create a UX brief for this football app\n+- I want to decide how list and details pages should look\n+- Help me design a reusable UI system for Razor views\n+- Figure out whether this app should feel more like an admin panel or a sports dashboard\n+- Interview me about UX preferences before generating frontend code\n+\n+## Example Response Shape\n+\n+A good result should look roughly like this:\n+\n+- Product Direction:\n+  Football-focused data dashboard for browsing clubs, players, matches, and ratings.\n+\n+- Confirmed Preferences:\n+  Dark theme, desktop-first, tables for lists, card-based details pages, restrained football branding.\n+\n+- Recommended Page Archetypes:\n+  Dashboard with summary cards, table-driven index pages, details pages with key info sections and related links.\n+\n+- Reusable Components:\n+  Shared navbar, page header, stat card, table shell, badges, breadcrumb, details info grid.\n+\n+- Open Questions:\n+  Whether forms should stay simple admin CRUD or become more guided.\n+\n+## Next-Step Guidance\n+\n+At the end, recommend one next step only.\n+\n+Examples:\n+- create a list-page skill from the approved brief\n+- create a details-page skill from the approved brief\n+- review existing views against the new UX brief\n+- define a shared component vocabulary before implementation\n*** End You do not generate full UI implementations unless explicitly asked by the parent agent. Your main purpose is to evaluate consistency, clarity, structure, and reuse potential across existing views.\n+\n+## Use This Agent For\n+\n+- reviewing existing Razor views and shared layout\n+- identifying repeated page patterns across index, details, and form pages\n+- finding inconsistencies in spacing, headings, actions, tables, cards, badges, or navigation\n+- spotting missing reusable components or partial-view candidates\n+- checking whether the current UI matches an approved UX brief or design direction\n+- suggesting the next best UI-focused action without implementing it\n+\n+## Do Not Use This Agent For\n+\n+- generating full page implementations from scratch\n+- changing backend code, routing, EF, or database structure\n+- inventing a new visual identity without user preferences or an approved brief\n+- broad product strategy unrelated to the existing UI\n+\n+## Constraints\n+\n+- Prefer reviewing existing files over speculating.\n+- Keep findings grounded in the current MVC and Razor structure.\n+- Separate confirmed issues from optional recommendations.\n+- Prefer consistency and reuse over novelty.\n+- If a pattern is repeated, call out the likely reusable component or partial.\n+\n+## Review Approach\n+\n+1. Inspect the shared layout, navigation, and any shared styling surfaces.\n+2. Inspect representative list, details, and form views if they exist.\n+3. Compare repeated page structures across entities.\n+4. Identify visual and interaction inconsistencies.\n+5. Summarize component opportunities, UX risks, and the single best next step.\n+\n+## What To Look For\n+\n+Audit against these dimensions when relevant:\n+\n+- page hierarchy and readability\n+- heading and section consistency\n+- primary and secondary action placement\n+- table versus card usage\n+- detail page structure and information grouping\n+- navigation clarity and back-link patterns\n+- visual density and spacing consistency\n+- responsive behavior clues in markup and classes\n+- repeated markup that should become a partial or reusable pattern\n+- mismatch between current UI and intended app identity\n+\n+## Output Format\n+\n+Return a concise audit with these sections:\n+\n+- Scope\n+- Findings\n+- Reusable Pattern Opportunities\n+- UX Risks or Gaps\n+- Recommended Next Step\n+- Relevant Files\n+\n+## Output Rules\n+\n+- Findings should be concrete and tied to files when possible.\n+- Prefer short bullets over long prose.\n+- Label subjective suggestions as recommendations, not defects.\n+- If the UI is already coherent, say so clearly.\n+- Recommend only one next step at the end.\n+\n+## Example Tasks\n+\n+- Audit the current MVC views for UI consistency.\n+- Compare index pages and tell me what should be standardized.\n+- Review the details pages and identify missing reusable patterns.\n+- Check whether the current shared layout supports a consistent design system.\n+- Review the current UI against an approved dark sports dashboard direction.\n*** End Patch","explanation":"Add a custom workspace sub-agent for UI review and UX audit with a narrow scope, minimal tools, and a concrete output format."},"tool_use_id":"call_q5Na8zrU7sufr8wZTeKywjaY__vscode-1777798830343","cwd":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\Desktop\\C#\\FutScores"}

Keywords: frontend, UI, UX, from scratch, greenfield, discovery, design brief, Razor views, MVC, layout, navigation, list page, details page, form page, design system.\n+tools: [read, search]\n+argument-hint: Define a fresh UI/UX direction from scratch before building frontend pages.\n+---\n+\n+You are a frontend UI and UX discovery agent for an ASP.NET Core MVC application.\n+\n+Your job is to define the UI direction before implementation starts. Treat the project as a greenfield frontend exercise unless the user explicitly tells you to reuse existing UI decisions. Existing views or CSS may exist in the repository, but you must not treat them as the design baseline.\n+\n+## Use This Agent For\n+\n+- starting the frontend UI and UX direction from scratch\n+- interviewing the user about visual style, layout, information density, and interaction patterns\n+- turning vague product ideas into a practical UI/UX brief\n+- deciding how list, details, dashboard, and form pages should feel before coding\n+- defining reusable page archetypes and component vocabulary for later implementation\n+\n+## Do Not Use This Agent For\n+\n+- auditing the current UI for consistency issues\n+- preserving the current visual direction by default\n+- implementing full Razor views or CSS unless explicitly asked\n+- changing backend architecture, EF, routing, or database structure\n+\n+## Constraints\n+\n+- Ignore the existing UI and UX as a source of design truth unless the user asks to incorporate it.\n+- Ask focused discovery questions before proposing a solution.\n+- Separate confirmed preferences from your own recommendations.\n+- Keep recommendations realistic for an ASP.NET Core MVC and Razor workflow.\n+- Prefer reusable patterns over one-off page ideas.\n+\n+## Approach\n+\n+1. Clarify the product intent, main users, and the most important tasks.\n+2. Ask about theme, density, branding strength, list-page style, and details-page style.\n+3. Clarify desktop versus mobile priority and whether the app should feel more like an admin panel, sports dashboard, or showcase experience.\n+4. If needed, inspect models, routes, or entity structure to understand the domain, but do not anchor on current UI markup.\n+5. Produce a concise UI/UX brief that can guide later implementation or later review.\n+\n+## Output Format\n+\n+Return a concise brief with these sections:\n+\n+- Product Direction\n+- Confirmed Preferences\n+- Inferred Recommendations\n+- Page Archetypes\n+- Reusable Components\n+- Interaction Principles\n+- Constraints\n+- Open Questions\n+- Recommended Next Step\n+\n+## Output Rules\n+\n+- Ask 6 to 8 practical questions before finalizing the brief when key preferences are missing.\n+- Make it explicit which points came from the user and which are your recommendations.\n+- Keep the brief implementation-ready, not abstract.\n+- Recommend only one next step at the end.\n+\n+## Example Tasks\n+\n+- Help me define the frontend UI and UX for this MVC app from scratch.\n+- Ignore the current styling and create a fresh UI direction for these Razor pages.\n+- Interview me and produce a UI brief before we implement the frontend.\n+- Decide how index, details, and form pages should look before writing code.\n*** End Patch","explanation":"Add a new workspace custom agent focused on greenfield frontend UI/UX discovery rather than auditing the existing interface."},"tool_use_id":"call_V2Nh1eHRSA8rB0FJ12PHSGdu__vscode-1777798830365","cwd":"c:\\Users\\Fran\\Desktop\\C#\\FutScores"}

public class HomeController : Controller\n     {\n         private readonly ILogger<HomeController> _logger;\n+        private readonly LeagueMockRepository _leagueRepository;\n         private readonly ClubMockRepository _clubRepository;\n         private readonly PlayerMockRepository _playerRepository;\n         private readonly MatchMockRepository _matchRepository;\n         private readonly RatingMockRepository _ratingRepository;\n \n         public HomeController(\n             ILogger<HomeController> logger,\n+            LeagueMockRepository leagueRepository,\n             ClubMockRepository clubRepository,\n             PlayerMockRepository playerRepository,\n             MatchMockRepository matchRepository,\n             RatingMockRepository ratingRepository)\n         {\n             _logger = logger;\n+            _leagueRepository = leagueRepository;\n             _clubRepository = clubRepository;\n             _playerRepository = playerRepository;\n             _matchRepository = matchRepository;\n             _ratingRepository = ratingRepository;\n         }\n@@\n         public IActionResult Index()\n         {\n+            var leagues = _leagueRepository.GetAll();\n             var clubs = _clubRepository.GetAll();\n             var players = _playerRepository.GetAll();\n             var matches = _matchRepository.GetAll();\n             var ratings = _ratingRepository.GetAll();\n+\n+            var recentMatches = matches\n+                .OrderByDescending(match => match.Date)\n+                .Take(6)\n+                .Select((match, index) =>\n+                {\n+                    var league = leagues.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Id == match.LeagueId);\n+                    var homeClub = clubs.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Id == match.HomeTeamId);\n+                    var awayClub = clubs.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Id == match.AwayTeamId);\n+\n+                    var statusLabel = index == 0 ? \"Featured\" : index < 3 ? \"Final\" : \"Recent\";\n+                    var statusTone = index == 0 ? \"live\" : index < 3 ? \"final\" : \"recent\";\n+\n+                    return new DashboardMatchCard\n+                    {\n+                        MatchId = match.Id,\n+                        LeagueName = league?.Name ?? \"Unknown League\",\n+                        HomeTeamName = homeClub?.Name ?? \"Unknown Home Team\",\n+                        AwayTeamName = awayClub?.Name ?? \"Unknown Away Team\",\n+                        HomeGoals = match.HomeGoals,\n+                        AwayGoals = match.AwayGoals,\n+                        Kickoff = match.Date,\n+                        KickoffLabel = match.Date.ToString(\"MMM dd, yyyy\"),\n+                        StatusLabel = statusLabel,\n+                        StatusTone = statusTone\n+                    };\n+                })\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            var featuredPlayers = ratings\n+                .GroupBy(rating => rating.PlayerId)\n+                .Select(group =>\n+                {\n+                    var player = players.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Id == group.Key);\n+                    var club = player != null ? clubs.FirstOrDefault(item => item.Id == player.ClubId) : null;\n+\n+                    return new DashboardFeaturedPlayer\n+                    {\n+                        PlayerId = group.Key,\n+                        FullName = player != null ? $\"{player.FirstName} {player.LastName}\" : \"Unknown Player\",\n+                        ClubName = club?.Name ?? \"Unknown Club\",\n+                        Position = player?.Position ?? Position.Midfielder,\n+                        Nationality = player?.Nationality ?? \"Unknown\",\n+                        AverageRating = Math.Round(group.Average(item => (double)item.Score), 1)\n+                    };\n+                })\n+                .OrderByDescending(player => player.AverageRating)\n+                .ThenBy(player => player.FullName)\n+                .Take(4)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            var searchShortcuts = new List<DashboardSearchShortcut>\n+            {\n+                new() { Label = \"Matches\", Controller = \"Match\", Action = \"Index\" },\n+                new() { Label = \"Players\", Controller = \"Player\", Action = \"Index\" },\n+                new() { Label = \"Clubs\", Controller = \"Club\", Action = \"Index\" },\n+                new() { Label = \"Leagues\", Controller = \"League\", Action = \"Index\" }\n+            };\n \n             var clubForms = clubs.Select(club =>\n             {\n                 var clubMatches = matches\n@@\n             var viewModel = new HomeViewModel\n             {\n                 TotalClubs = clubs.Count(),\n                 TotalPlayers = players.Count(),\n                 TotalMatches = matches.Count(),\n                 AverageRating = ratings.Any() ? Math.Round(ratings.Average(r => (double)r.Score), 1) : 0,\n+                RecentMatches = recentMatches,\n+                FeaturedPlayers = featuredPlayers,\n+                SearchShortcuts = searchShortcuts,\n                 ClubForms = clubForms\n             };\n \n             return View(viewModel);\n         }\n*** Update File: mobileMenu.hasAttribute(\"hidden\");\n+\n+            if (isHidden) {\n+                mobileMenu.removeAttribute(\"hidden\");\n+                mobileToggle.setAttribute(\"aria-expanded\", \"true\");\n+            } else {\n+                mobileMenu.setAttribute(\"hidden\", \"hidden\");\n+                mobileToggle.setAttribute(\"aria-expanded\", \"false\");\n+            }\n+ HTMLSelectElement) || !(queryInput instanceof HTMLInputElement)) {\n+                return;\n+            }\n+\n+            const scope = scopeInput.value || \"Match\";\n+            const query = queryInput.value.trim();\n+            const targetUrl = query\n+                ? `/${scope}/Index?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}`\n+                : `/${scope}/Index`;\n+\n+
